text: 定義: Spermatic cord的pampiniform plexus dilation
Left predominant, 青少年比成人常見, 高瘦青少年較常見(gene related)
11-15% of the general population, 35% of men with primary infertility(最常造成男性不孕的已知原因, idiopathic還是最多)
佔了26% 男性不孕症主因

成因: 1.Internal spermatic vein(gonadal v.)沒有靜脈valve, 導致靜脈血易回流造成pampiniform plexus dilation 2.Left gonadal v.注入left renal vein接近90度, 血流阻力較大(所以left predominant) 3. Nutcracker phenomenom(Lt renal v.被aorta和SMA壓迫)4.後腹腔有腫瘤壓到gonadal v.(只有右側varicocele要小心)
造成testis傷害的病理機制: pampiniform plexus因為dilation喪失了熱交換機制所以無法使testis降溫, 繼而影響精子與睪固酮的製造, 另外回流不好也會缺氧並造成代謝物(prostaglandin)堆積, 造成oxidative stress上升, 精子DNA fragmentation, apoptosis
S/S: scrotal pain/heaviness, 坐下躺下會減緩, spermatic cord摸到bag of worms
Image: Confirmed by scrotal color Duplex(Dopplar紅轉藍+venous diameter>3.5 or 3.0cm)
Subclinical: not detected on PE; found by US

text: 人類的性別是由基因決定的，關鍵基因為：
SRY基因（位於Y染色體短臂）：其產物含有HMG box（能夠使DNA彎曲），進而改變基因表現，誘導睪丸形成 → 男性表徵。
DAX1基因（核荷爾蒙受體）：可抑制SRY下游誘導睪丸分化的基因。
WNT4基因：主要表現在卵巢，具有“抗睪丸（antitestis）”功能。
過去認為女性性別為“預設路徑”（即未表現SRY），但如今已知WNT4、DAX1等基因能主動誘導女性性腺發育。

text: Erection - Corpus Spongiosum and Glans Penis
The pressure in the corpus spongiosum and glans is only one third to one half that in the corpora cavernosa
Tunical covering is thin(only circular layer) over the corpus spongiosum and virtually absent over the glans, ensures minimal venous occlusion
During the full-erection phase, partial compression of the deep dorsal and circumflex veins between Buck fascia and the engorged corpora cavernosa
During the rigid-erection phase, the ischiocavernosus and bulbocavernosus muscles forcefully compress the spongiosum and penile veins

3.3.2.2.2 Recommendations for the diagnosis of Peyronie's disease
Recommendations
In the medical and sexual history of patients with Peyronie's disease, include duration of
Ithe disease, penile pain, change of penile deformity, difficulty in vaginal intromission due to
deformity, and erectile dysfunction (ED).
In the physical examination, include assessment of palpable plaques, penile length, extent
of curvature (self-photograph, vacuum-assisted erection test or pharmacological-induced
lerection) and any other possibly related diseases (Dupuytren's contracture, Ledderhose
disease).
Do not use Peyronie's disease specific questionnaire in everyday clinical practice.
Do not use ultrasound (US) measurement of plaque size in everyday clinical practice.
Use Doppler US only in the case of dliagnostic evaluation of ED, to ascertain vascular
parameters associated with ED.
